title: Archive Plan Cleanup Hardening
goal: Fix #323 so `archive-plan --apply-cleanup` removes the active compact state row for the archived plan and preserves unrelated roadmap lanes.
Add regression coverage for the observed `.plan.json` active-row and unrelated-roadmap-lane failure.
non_goals: Do not close the machine-first-planning-state lane.
Do not change legacy Markdown roadmap cleanup beyond the bug fix needed here.

intent.outcome: Make archive cleanup trustworthy for compact state.toml installs.
intent.constraints: Preserve unrelated roadmap lanes/candidates; remove only TODO rows that point at the archived plan; keep cleanup behavior narrow and regression-tested.
intent.latitude: Patch the smallest matching/merge bug in the planning package and add focused tests.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the fix requires changing archive semantics or broad roadmap matching policy.

execution.next_step: Archive this completed plan with `archive-plan --apply-cleanup`, close #323, and continue with #325.
execution.proof: Focused planning package tests reproduce and cover the active row removal plus unrelated lane preservation; full installer tests and ruff pass.

invariants: State cleanup preserves unrelated roadmap lanes.
A compact TODO row that uses `plan` or `surface` can be removed when it points at the archived plan.
Roadmap cleanup must not match on generic planning/workflow tokens alone.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: machine-first planning state and closeout hygiene

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue with #325 after archive cleanup is fixed

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Archive cleanup can be trusted again for compact state closeout.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Initial inspection found likely stale-state overwrite and over-broad token matching.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: archive cleanup now matches compact `plan` references, preserves TODO cleanup when roadmap cleanup also writes state, and ignores generic `planning` roadmap tokens
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: dogfood archive cleanup against this active plan, then run planning checks

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: fix the next first-lane trust blocker discovered by dogfooding
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: patch archive-plan compact cleanup so it removes the right active row and preserves unrelated roadmap lanes
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject broad archive rewrites; require a regression for the observed #323 shape

execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before broad archive-plan redesign or new cleanup modes
execution_bounds.stop before touching: GitHub issue state until tests pass

stop_conditions.stop when: focused regression tests and planning checks pass
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: fix requires changing roadmap continuation semantics
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work expands into product reconciliation or broader lane closure
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: regression cannot be reproduced or fixed narrowly

context_budget.live working set: archive_execplan, compact state TODO cleanup, roadmap cleanup token matching, focused installer tests
context_budget.recoverable later: older Markdown roadmap cleanup edge cases outside #323
context_budget.externalize before shift: the root cause and validation commands in this execplan
context_budget.tiny resumability note: fix #323 in installer.py; active rows may use `plan`, and roadmap cleanup must not match generic `planning` token
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after adding tests or before committing

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: fix archive-plan cleanup so closeout can be trusted
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: preserve unrelated roadmap lanes and remove only rows that point at the archived plan
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: the exact narrow helper changes and regression test shape
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the issue needs a broad archive cleanup redesign

active_milestone.scope: Patch and test compact state archive cleanup for #323.

immediate_next_action: Dogfood archive cleanup against this plan, then close #323 and continue with #325.
invariants: Only the archived plan's active/queued row is removed.
Unrelated roadmap lanes and candidates are preserved.
The fix is covered by focused package tests.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Compact state TODO references may use `plan` as well as `surface`.
Generic plan-stem tokens such as `planning` are not safe roadmap cleanup discriminators.
open_questions_to_close: Does the active row use `plan`, `surface`, or both?
Does roadmap cleanup overwrite TODO cleanup when both mutate state?
Which token caused unrelated lane removal?
completion_criteria: #323 reproduction is covered by a failing-then-passing regression.
`archive-plan --apply-cleanup` removes the matching compact active row even when it uses `plan`.
Roadmap cleanup preserves unrelated lanes/candidates when only generic plan tokens overlap.
Focused tests and planning checks pass.

execution_run.what happened: Patched archive cleanup so compact state rows using `plan` are recognized, TODO cleanup is merged with roadmap cleanup before writing state, and generic `planning` tokens do not remove unrelated roadmap lanes. Added a regression for the exact `.plan.json` active-row plus unrelated-lane failure.
execution_run.changed surfaces: planning active state and execplan; archive cleanup implementation; installer regression tests
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "archive_execplan_apply_cleanup"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py; rg -n "planning-backed-dogfooding-guardrail|archive-plan-cleanup-hardening|_merge_todo_state_from_toml_lines|_plan_stem_tokens" packages/planning .agentic-workspace/planning
execution_run.result for continuation: #323 implementation is complete; #325 remains the next first-lane follow-up.

proof_report.validation proof: `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "archive_execplan_apply_cleanup"`; `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q`; `uv run ruff check packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py`; `rg -n "planning-backed-dogfooding-guardrail|archive-plan-cleanup-hardening|_merge_todo_state_from_toml_lines|_plan_stem_tokens" packages/planning .agentic-workspace/planning`
proof_report.proof achieved now: Archive cleanup removes compact active rows that use `plan`, preserves that removal when roadmap cleanup also writes compact state, and preserves unrelated roadmap lanes when only generic `planning` tokens overlap.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The #323 bug itself is fixed and regression-tested, but the parent machine-first closeout-hygiene lane remains open.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Agentic Workspace should make agent closeout and continuation trustworthy through checked-in, machine-readable state.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: fix the package workflow defect before relying on archive cleanup again
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can archive cleanup be used without corrupting active state or roadmap continuation?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #323 is fixed, but #325 still owns product-level reconciliation for lower-trust closeouts.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: installer regression test plus this archived execplan
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen if archive cleanup again leaves active rows or removes unrelated lanes
